propositions: Existing tests only simulate compaction failures via Python exceptions or hand-built orphan states, never killing a real compactor process.
A SIGKILL mid-retirement skips rollback, lock-release, cache-cleanup, and git-staging code, leaving the real durability contract unproven.
Add a deterministic subprocess test that SIGKILLs a real rebar compact process paused after the first source rename via a failpoint/filesystem barrier.
From a fresh CLI process, recovery must reclaim the write lock, preserve exact pre-crash semantic state, lose no source UUID, and yield a clean or repairable fsck result.
Re-running compaction after crash must be idempotent or perform the documented repair, after which fsck is clean.
The test must be deterministic and not rely on a narrow timing window.
